← the Atlas

Graduating mini-ci — a live CI service vs. justci's batch translator

feature · seedling ·proposed ·JUN 06, 2026

mini-ci proves that ssh can carry a live, typed, reactive surface instead of a batch artifact — turning the CI runner from a translator you scrape into a service you attach to. That inversion wins the watch-a-run half of CI outright and graduates, drishti-style, into its own repo. It still owes the trigger-and-gate half that is justci's reason to exist. An honest plan for both.

Contents

  • The reframe — a batch translator vs. a live service
  • What mini-ci already is
  • The half it doesn’t replace — trigger, identity, trust
  • The reckoning — parity to reach, and what only a live service has
    • Reaching parity — the honest debt
    • Past parity — what falls out of the inverted shape
  • Graduating to a standalone repo

A proposal to grow packages/surface/example/mini-ci — today a falsifiability test for “an interactive TUI over oRPC stdio” — into a feature-rich, standalone CI tool that takes over from juspay/justci, delivered the way remote-process-monitor graduated into srid/drishti. Every load-bearing claim below was checked against both codebases. 22-agent workflow · adversarial verification

Status: proposed · maturity seedling · substrate shipped in mini-ci ( #1076 · known TUI raw-mode bug )

Headline

justci and mini-ci are not the same program at two maturities — they are two categories of program, separated by what crosses the ssh wire. justci ships an opaque batch artifact (a git bundle in, scraped .log files out) and reconstructs “liveness” out-of-band through a separate socket client. mini-ci ships a live typed reactive surface in-band over oRPC stdio, so the runner stops being a translator that emits a run and becomes a service that owns the DAG as state — one you attach, detach, and reconnect to. That single inversion replaces the watch-a-run half of CI outright and is generative: a browser dashboard, an MCP server for agent CLIs, multi-platform fan-in, typed rerun, and self-healing reconnect fall out as composition of packages kolu already ships. But it still owes the trigger-and-gate half that is justci’s actual reason to exist — forge-event ingestion, per-commit run identity, commit-status output, and a trust model for a now-remotely-mutable service. “Replaces justci and does more” is true on the observation axis and a concrete, costed backlog on the gating axis. This note owns both.

The reframe — a batch translator vs. a live service

justci’s own dump-yaml is the confession of category: it proves the pipeline assembly — a project’s just DAG ([metadata("ci")] root) into a single process-compose document — is a pure function, fed a SHA placeholder so it runs offline outside a git checkout. The run is the process; there is no serve-only mode. ssh is then a dumb, one-way, per-node pipe: a git bundle (plus a nix-store export of the runner derivation) goes out to set the node up; each recipe node re-ssh’s to just --no-deps; and only an exit code comes back. Per-recipe stdout is redirected to .ci/<sha>/<plat>/<recipe>.log files, and the progress feed carries the on-disk path, never the bytes — a later consumer reads them off disk.

“Liveness,” then, is something justci reconstructs out of band. status / monitor / logs are thin passthroughs to a separate, baked process-compose client dialed at .ci/pc.sock; justci’s own guard dies before it even reaches the socket when no run is in progress. The introspection is a side-channel that only exists while a batch lives.

mini-ci moves the one load-bearing axis: ssh carries a typed, schema-validated, snapshot-then-delta surface (oRPC stdio). The runner’s nodes Cell and per-node nodeLog Stream are in-band, over the same surface a client attaches to — observable whether or not a run is mid-flight, with no second binary and no pc.sock. Everything “more” in this note is downstream of that one change.

What mini-ci already is

This is what just run srid1 paints today — a live dashboard in your terminal, driven entirely by the runner’s nodes Cell and the attached node’s nodeLog Stream over ssh stdio:

mini-ci — just run srid1
$ just run srid1
» host: srid1 (system=x86_64-linux)
» runner drv: /nix/store/p4z…-mini-ci-runner.drv
mini-ci · remote-process-monitor srid1 ● connected
# node status exit time
1 surface ✓ ok 0 2.1s
2 nix-host ✓ ok 0 1.8s
3▸ monitor ● running · 4.3s
── monitor ───────────────────────────────────────────
> tsc --noEmit (142 files)
checked src/agent/main.ts … ok
checked src/client/wire.ts … ok
▍
[1-3] attach n/p cycle r rerun q quit

The honest floor: mini-ci is a proven substrate, not a product. A long-lived runner owns a task DAG — each node a child process with a per-node log buffer — and serves it as a @kolu/surface over stdio. The entire tool is three primitives (src/common/surface.ts):

PrimitivePathWhat it carries
Cellsurface.nodes.get({})The whole pipeline’s node-state — snapshot then deltas. ↔ a list view.
Streamsurface.nodeLog.get({ id })One node’s output: a buffered snapshot frame, then append deltas (a discriminated union, so a late subscriber replays a finished node from the top). ↔ attach.
Proceduresurface.node.rerun({ id })The only mutation: reset the node + its transitive dependents to pending and reschedule. ↔ input.

It reaches the host the drishti way via @kolu/surface-nix-host’s HostSession: nix copy the prebuilt runner .drv to the host, nix-store --realise it, then ssh host mini-ci-runner --stdio; localhost skips the copy. HostSession owns the ref-count, reconnect, and a copying → connecting → connected state cell — the same session for local and remote, only the transport differs.

mini-ci.test.ts drives the real runner surface through the real stdio codec (createLoopbackPair → serveOverStdio → stdioLink, byte-identical to the ssh path; the runner spawns real child processes — nothing is mocked), so a green run is genuine evidence. It banks:

  • Cell snapshot-then-delta — a late subscriber’s first frame is the current state.
  • Race-free topo order — across every captured frame, a node runs only once its dependency is ok.
  • Per-node log replay — a late subscriber to a finished node gets its buffered output as the first snapshot.
  • Rerun re-runs the closure — node.rerun resets the node + dependents and they settle ok again.
  • No false greens — a failed dependency skips its dependents.

Deliberately not justci — today

TUI-only (no browser client tree, no surface-app); a singular implementSurface over one HostSession; a read-only /nix/store closure, so the default pipeline is typecheck-only by construction (tsc --noEmit over a real dependency closure); a hand-written ci.json; and client-side detach/reattach only — runner-side state dies with the runner or its ssh pipe. These are the gaps the rest of this note prices, not hidden costs.

The half it doesn’t replace — trigger, identity, trust

A CI tool is defined at least as much by its front half as its execution engine: what event starts a run, against which immutable commit, and how a verdict reaches a developer who isn’t watching. justci’s reason to exist is that loop — push → forge event → run pinned to a worktree SHA → commit-status the forge can gate a PR on. mini-ci’s verified strengths are all about a developer who is attached and watching. The reframe must not overreach into the half it doesn’t touch.

  • Trigger. mini-ci has no webhook / poll / push ingestion and no PR-event story. A live service you attach to is orthogonal to the event that kicks a run. Liveness answers how do I watch a run, not how does a run begin.
  • Run identity & history. justci is per-SHA by design (.ci/<sha>/…, an immutable HEAD pin per commit — an append-only ledger of runs over commits). mini-ci owns one live, mutable DAG; node.rerun overwrites the prior result in place. There is no model for which commit this run is or where prior runs went — a missing data model, deeper than durability.
  • Trust. node.rerun is remote code execution by construction, over a long-lived, attachable surface. justci’s batch model has a bounded attack surface (ssh in, run a sealed batch, ssh out). mini-ci has zero authn/authz and no read-observer-vs-mutator split — fine for a TUI over your own ssh, load-bearing the instant a multi-client dashboard is claimed.
  • Async delivery & concurrent mutation. justci’s deliverable is a status badge the forge sees with nothing attached. mini-ci’s story is all pull — attach and witness. And two clients both calling node.rerun, or rerunning a running node, has no defined serialization or run ownership. Multi-client is an affordance with a coordination cost, not pure upside.

So is it a replacement?

It replaces the watch-a-run half outright and does genuinely more there. On the trigger-and-gate half it is a plan, not a replacement — and for canonical “run once on a commit, post a verdict, walk away,” justci’s strict batch (dirty-tree refusal, immutable worktree SHA, fire-and-forget status) may simply be the right shape. The live service enables a different, additional class of workflow (interactive, agent-driven, reattachable); it is not strictly superior. The novelty is real against justci specifically — a small Haskell batch tool — not against the hosted mainstream (GitHub Actions, Buildkite, Dagger) that already streams logs and reattaches. The durable edge is the substrate: a typed, reconnecting, composable surface over your own ssh, no hosted runner.

The reckoning — parity to reach, and what only a live service has

Reaching parity — the honest debt

Capability mini-ci must grow to stand in for justci. harder = net-new design + code on a proven substrate; open = unbuilt on either side. “The framework can do it” is not “mini-ci does it.”

justci capabilitysurface-native pathStatus
Trigger: push → forge event → run on a pinned SHANo analog. Needs ingestion + commit-pinning at trigger time. The largest unscoped pillar.harder
Per-SHA run identity, history, append-only ledgerA run-identity data model; today one mutable DAG, rerun overwrites in place.harder
GitHub commit-statuses <recipe>@<platform> + protectA terminal-transition side effect reading the nodes Cell + a branch-protection writer. The surface makes state observable, not forge integration free.harder
Async verdict delivery (a badge seen with nothing attached)Net-new notify / webhook-out / badge axis; a live dashboard is additive, not a substitute.harder
Bounded trust modelauthn/authz + a read-observer vs. mutator split on a remotely-mutable surface.harder
Real builds (write-heavy: vite build, nix build)A writable scratch workspace (--workspace / MINI_CI_WORKSPACE) or nix-build-as-task; the read-only /nix/store closure is typecheck-only by construction. The load-bearing execution gap. Surface contract unchanged — builds are just more nodes.harder
just-DAG ingestion + dump-yaml / graphA just → PipelineSpec translator; mini-ci reads a hand-written ci.json today.harder
Strict-by-default hygiene (dirty-tree refuse, HEAD pin, per-sha logs)Net-new for a gating tool; mini-ci runs whatever cwd it’s handed.harder
Durability across observer/process churnWorse today: runner-side state dies with the pipe. Server-restart survival is the daemon plan’s job.harder
Caching / artifact passing between nodesnix-build-as-task inherits Nix’s cache; otherwise net-new. (justci has roadmap gaps here too.)open
Per-recipe [linux]/[macos] filterA per-node platform predicate in PipelineSpec. justci’s one admitted README roadmap item (kolu’s ci/mod.just notes it: a [linux]-only step is still scheduled on macOS and fails today).open

Past parity — what falls out of the inverted shape

Affordances justci structurally cannot match today — each tied to a specific justci limit, each a build step on the proven substrate, not a wish. Here is the payoff made visible — the same surface wears three faces (TUI · web · MCP), each a thin client adapter over the one implementSurface router, only the link differs. First, in the browser: the proposed sakshi PWA, fanning N platform runners in to one live view — colour is real-time node status, the panel streams the selected node’s log:

sakshi.srid.ca / juspay/kolu
sakshijuspay/kolu26d2c2d 3 lanes · connected
x86_64-linuxaarch64-darwinaarch64-linuxnix✓ 3m40✓ 4m02● 1m12e2e✓ 2m03● 1m55⏸ —unit✓ 0m31✓ 0m44⏸ —lint✓ 0m12✓ 0m14⏸ —
aarch64-darwin · e2e  ● running 1m55s⟳ rerun
cucumber · 14 scenarios ✓ open a terminal … 2.1s ✓ split a pane … 1.4s ▸ reconnect after server restart …▍

And the same three primitives, to an agent. Because the runner already serves the surface over a byte pipe (serveOverStdio), an MCP server is just another serve target — a thin oRPC→MCP adapter (no @orpc/mcp package exists; it’s ~one file over the official MCP SDK) that maps node.rerun to an MCP tool, the nodes Cell and nodeLog Stream to subscribable resources — with get_nodes / tail_log tool mirrors, since agent CLIs surface tools more reliably than resource subscriptions — and adds a wait_for_settle verdict tool. Declare it in any agent CLI over stdio, the same transport the TUI already rides:

// .mcp.json (proposed) — Claude Code; Codex, opencode & Gemini CLI take the same shape
{ "mcpServers": { "mini-ci": {
  "type": "stdio", "command": "npx",
  "args": ["-y", "@kolu/mini-ci-mcp", "--host", "srid1"] } } }
claude — CI over the mini-ci MCP server
$ claude # mini-ci declared in .mcp.json — stdio, over ssh
# /mcp → mini-ci · 4 tools: get_nodes tail_log rerun_node wait_for_settle
user: kolu CI went red on linux — find the broken node, show why, rerun it.
→ get_nodes() # surface.nodes.get({})
surface ✓ ok exit 0 2.1s
nix-host ✓ ok exit 0 1.8s
monitor ✗ failed exit 1 4.3s
→ tail_log({ id: "monitor" }) # surface.nodeLog.get({ id })
> tsc --noEmit (142 files)
src/client/wire.ts:88 — TS2345: 'string' not assignable to 'NodeId'
assistant: monitor failed on a tsc error at wire.ts:88. Fixed the cast.
→ rerun_node({ id: "monitor" }) # surface.node.rerun({ id }) — only mutation
{ ok: true } # resets monitor + dependents, reschedules
→ wait_for_settle({ timeoutMs: 120000 }) # blocks on the live Cell
{ passed: true, failed: [], durationMs: 4100 }
assistant: Green ✓ — monitor passed in 4.1s. The wire.ts fix cleared it.
mini-ci surfaceMCP primitive
nodes Cell (snapshot-then-delta)ci://pipeline resource (subscribe → updated) + a get_nodes() tool mirrorA Cell is read-current-then-get-notified — a subscribable resource’s exact twin.
nodeLog Stream ({id})node://{id}/log resource template + a tail_log({id}) cursor toolThe {id}-parameterised Stream maps 1:1 to a URI template; the MAX_LOG_CHARS clamp is a natural bounded tail.
node.rerun Procedurererun_node({id}) tooloRPC procedures → MCP tools 1:1 (Zod → JSON Schema). Where authz must live — this is RCE.
(derived for agents)wait_for_settle() → verdict, cancel() toolsCollapse the frame-by-frame watch into one await — justci’s end-of-run exit code, on the live DAG, on demand.

This is the agent-driven CI the live shape makes natural — and the one thing justci reaches for but its model forecloses: #22 · MCP server for ci, done properly is justci’s own design note for an agent-controlled MCP mode; it built it and reverted it ( #18 · reverted in the same PR ) because, in a batch translator, launching the server auto-ran every recipe — process-compose has no serve-only / --no-start mode, and its MCP surface is “request/response only, no streaming.” A runner that owns the DAG as idle state has that separation by construction, and nodeLog’s snapshot-then-delta is the streaming event source #22 can’t get. (Proposed, not built — the MCP face is a designed third serve target on the same router; and rerun_node is RCE, so it must land behind the Phase 1 authz boundary.)

Live-service affordanceThe justci limit it answersStatus
In-band introspection (nodes Cell + nodeLog Stream) over the attach surfaceA separate baked pc client on .ci/pc.sock that dies when no run is livebeyond
Per-node log replay as a live stream for late subscribersStdout scraped to .ci/<sha>/<plat>/<recipe>.log files, the feed carrying only the pathbeyond
Typed rerun of a node + its transitive dependents on the live DAG (no new process)justci run RECIPE@PLATFORM spins up a brand-new process-compose batchbeyond
Self-healing reconnect: every get() re-yields a fresh snapshot; STREAM_RETRY.retry = Infinity; HostSession respawns the childNo reattach to an in-flight run (introspection only via the side socket)beyond
Idle attach + selective start (clients attach before start()) #22 · no serve-only mode — process-compose has no idle subcommand. Fixable-pending-upstream, not impossible — but not the default shape.beyond
Three faces over one surface: the shipped TUI, a proposed PWA dashboard, and a proposed MCP server — each a thin client adapter over the same implementSurface router, only the link differsNo live dashboard and no agent-drivable API; the only feed is the baked pc clientbeyond
Agent-driven CI: nodes→get_nodes, nodeLog→tail_log, rerun→rerun_node, + a wait_for_settle verdict — an MCP server is just another serve target, carrying the snapshot-then-delta event source MCP’s own request/response surface lacks #22 · MCP server for ci — justci wants this, tried + reverted it ( #18 ); launch auto-ran every recipe, and process-compose has no serve-only modebeyond
Fan-IN: N platform runners serving the same surface, multiplexed as key-namespaced siblings via implementSurfacesFan-OUT: one ssh + one .log + one status context per (recipe × platform) pair, plus a #47 · sharding aggregator hackopen
Typed cancel / cron / mid-run reload proceduresSealed at preflight, pinned to a worktree SHA — foreclosed under the translatoropen
Concurrent-client mutation semantics (serialization, run ownership)A non-question under one-run-one-driver — and a new problem the live shape creates, which it must ownopen

(Topo-ordered DAG execution with cascade-skip is already at parity — proven race-free in mini-ci.test.ts.)

Graduating to a standalone repo

The delivery vehicle is de-risked by precedent. remote-process-monitor — a full agent/client/common/server browser tree inside kolu — graduated into srid/drishti by adopting @kolu/surface + surface-nix-host first, then surface-app later ( #47 · freshness by composition ). The @kolu/* packages are npins-pinned by the Nix callers and hydrated by cp -rL (not symlinks, for TypeScript’s transitive imports); drishti’s KOLU_AGENT_DRV is the exact analog of mini-ci’s MINI_CI_RUNNER_DRV. mini-ci’s own README already names the path: “mini-ci could graduate to its own repo the way remote-process-monitor became drishti.”

License is determined, not chosen. surface and surface-nix-host are MIT, but surface-app (needed for the PWA dashboard) is AGPL-3.0-or-later — already mini-ci’s package.json license, so adoption forces nothing new.

It clears the electricity bar. The ①②③ test — domain-agnostic / hides hard volatility / graduates with a different consumer — is already met by the wire/ssh stack: electricity.mdx lists @kolu/surface, surface-nix-host, and surface-app as electricities with drishti as the proof-of-③ second consumer. What graduates here is the product (mini-ci → its own repo), riding receptacles that already graduated. The two-way street: reusable shell concerns (strict hygiene, the trigger model, run identity) extracted from the new repo harden kolu’s libraries back.

Naming open — srid’s product line follows a single Sanskrit perception-word (drishti = दृष्टि, “sight”), not his descriptive-English library names (omnix, nixci). The thesis is witnessing a live build, so:

  • sakshi (साक्षी, “witness / the one who observes”) — strongest fit; “sakshi watched the build” reads naturally; parallel in shape to drishti.
  • prahari (प्रहरी, “sentinel / one who keeps watch”) — matches the long-lived-runner / ephemeral-clients model precisely.
  • netra (नेत्र, “eye”) — shortest, a direct sibling to drishti’s “sight.”

Lead with sakshi; the choice is srid’s. (A witness-name slightly undersells the eventual gatekeeper scope once the trigger half lands — acceptable, since live witnessing is the differentiator.)

  • ✓ Phase 0 · the falsifiability testShipped: a long-lived runner owns the DAG and serves nodes Cell + nodeLog Stream + node.rerun over the real stdio transport. mini-ci.test.ts banks snapshot-then-delta, race-free topo order, late-subscriber log replay, rerun-the-closure, and no-false-greens; live e2e just run localhost --json typechecks a real closure over ssh stdio. TUI-only, singular implementSurface, read-only closure ⇒ typecheck-only.
  • ○ Phase 1 · parityPay the debits across both halves. Execution: writable-workspace builds, a just → PipelineSpec translator + dump-yaml/graph, strict-by-default hygiene. Front half: a trigger model pinned to an immutable SHA, per-SHA run identity/history, GitHub commit-statuses + a protect equivalent, and a minimal authz boundary on node.rerun.
  • ○ Phase 2 · beyondThe live-service affordances: light the surface-app PWA dashboard and the MCP server face — three faces over one surface (TUI · web · MCP), the MCP face a thin oRPC→MCP adapter behind the Phase 1 authz boundary (since rerun_node is RCE); wire the plural layer for multi-platform fan-in with defined concurrent-mutation/run-ownership semantics; idle-attach + selective start; typed cancel/cron/reload; per-recipe platform filter; async result delivery. Price the idle-runner lifecycle against kolu’s own warm pu-box pool (flock-leased per run).
  • ○ Phase 3 · graduationGraduate exactly as remote-process-monitor became drishti: npins-pin @kolu/* + hydrate by cp -rL, thread MINI_CI_RUNNER_DRV, adopt surface-app, ship a drishti-style flake / justfile / home-manager service. Extract reusable concerns back into the kolu libraries. Pick the name.
← the Atlas github ↗
